questions.question: Which of the following algorithms is an extension of the Nearest-Neighbor Learning algorithm?
questions.explanation: The Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm is an extension of the Nearest-Neighbor Learning algorithm, where the weights are assigned based on the distance from the samples to the data points.

questions.question: Nearest Centroid Classifier is based on
questions.explanation: Nearest Centroid Classifier is based on Euclidean Distance. It works by assigning each new data point to the class whose centroid is closest.

questions.question: The Locally Weighted algorithm is based on
questions.explanation: The Locally Weighted algorithm is based on Locally Weighted Regression. It works by assigning different weights to different neighbors based on their distance and their influence on the local regression.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm over the Nearest-Neighbor Learning algorithm?
options: A) It can handle high-dimensional data
B) It can handle non-linear relationships
C) It can assign weights to different neighbors based on their distance
questions.explanation: The main advantage of Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor Algorithm over the Nearest-Neighbor Learning algorithm is that it can assign weights to different neighbors based on their distance, which can improve the accuracy of the algorithm.

questions.question: Which algorithm is used for classification?
questions.explanation: Nearest Centroid Classifier is used for classification. It works by assigning each new data point to the class whose centroid is closest. This algorithm is typically used for binary classification problems.
